title: The 11 Best Shipping Software for Ecommerce
subtitle: A ranked analysis of the top platforms for ecommerce businesses to compare carrier rates, print labels in bulk, and automate fulfillment workflows.
audience: Online stores managing shipping and labels

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Carrier Integrations & Discounts (30%), Platform & Marketplace Integrations (25%), Core Feature Set (20%), Ease of Use & Support (15%), and Scalability & Automation (10%).

answer_capsule: The best shipping software for ecommerce is ShipStation, followed by Shippo and Easyship.

criteria.name: Carrier Integrations & Discounts
criteria.description: Breadth of available carriers (national and international) and the quality of pre-negotiated shipping rate discounts.

criteria.name: Platform & Marketplace Integrations
criteria.description: Ability to connect with ecommerce platforms (Shopify, BigCommerce), marketplaces (Amazon, Etsy), and other business systems.

criteria.name: Core Feature Set
criteria.description: Effectiveness of core functions like batch label printing, rate comparison, address validation, returns management, and tracking pages.

criteria.name: Ease of Use & Support
criteria.description: Intuitive user interface, quality of onboarding, and responsiveness of customer support channels (phone, chat, email).

criteria.name: Scalability & Automation
criteria.description: Features that support growing volume, such as automation rules, multi-user access, and inventory management capabilities.

query_intents: best shipping software for small business

guide.a: Ecommerce shipping software is a tool that connects your online store to multiple shipping carriers like USPS, UPS, and FedEx. It allows you to manage orders from all your sales channels in one place, compare shipping rates, print labels in bulk, and automate tracking notifications to customers. Its primary purpose is to save time and money on fulfillment.

guide.q: How does shipping software save money?
guide.a: Shipping software saves money in two main ways. First, it provides access to pre-negotiated, discounted shipping rates that are lower than what you would pay at the post office or a carrier's retail location. Second, it automates manual tasks, reducing labor costs associated with copying and pasting addresses, comparing rates, and sending tracking information.

guide.q: What should I look for when choosing a provider?
guide.a: Focus on four key areas. Check that it integrates with your ecommerce platform (e.g., Shopify, Amazon). Ensure it supports the carriers you need to use. Evaluate its automation features to see how much time it can save you. Finally, consider the pricing model and whether it aligns with your monthly shipping volume.

how_to_choose: Start with your sales channels. Your top priority is a tool that integrates directly with the platforms and marketplaces where you sell.
Estimate your monthly shipping volume. This will determine which pricing tier you fall into and whether a provider with a generous free plan (like Pirate Ship or Veeqo) is a good fit.
Identify your most common shipping destinations. If you ship internationally frequently, prioritize a platform like Easyship that specializes in calculating duties and taxes.

Evaluate your need for automation. If you're shipping more than 20-30 orders a day, look for features like 'if-this-then-that' rules that can automatically select the cheapest carrier or apply specific packaging.

faqs.q: What is the cheapest shipping software?
faqs.a: Pirate Ship is the cheapest option for US-based sellers using USPS and UPS, as it has no monthly fees. For multi-carrier needs, Veeqo (owned by Amazon) is currently completely free, and Shippo offers a free pay-per-label plan.

faqs.q: Does Shopify have its own shipping software?
faqs.a: Yes, Shopify has a built-in tool called Shopify Shipping. It provides discounted rates from carriers like USPS, UPS, and DHL and is integrated directly into the Shopify admin. However, third-party shipping software often offers more features, more carrier options, and better automation for stores with higher volume.

faqs.q: Can shipping software handle international orders?
faqs.a: Yes, most shipping software can handle international orders by helping you print labels and fill out customs forms electronically. Platforms like Easyship specialize in this, offering features like pre-calculating duties and taxes to show customers at checkout.

faqs.q: What's the difference between shipping software and a 3PL?
faqs.a: Shipping software is a tool you use to manage and ship orders from your own location. A Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider is a company you outsource your entire fulfillment process to; you send them your inventory, and they pick, pack, and ship orders for you from their warehouse.

honest_disclosures: Most providers on this list are optimized for US-based merchants and carriers. Businesses based in Europe or Asia should give extra weight to providers like Sendcloud or Easyship.
Advertised shipping discounts can vary. The final rate depends on package size, weight, destination, and your specific account volume.

glossary.definition: A company that buys shipping services in bulk from major carriers (like USPS, UPS, FedEx) and resells them to lower-volume shippers at a discount. Most shipping software acts as a shipping aggregator to provide users with better rates than they could get on their own.

entries.name: ShipStation
entries.best_for: Growing ecommerce businesses that need to connect multiple sales channels and carriers into a single, automated workflow.
entries.verdict: Ranks #1 for its unmatched number of integrations, connecting to over 100 marketplaces, carriers, and systems, making it the most versatile hub for growing ecommerce stores.
entries.verdict_short: The best all-around platform for integrations and automation, supporting growth across multiple channels.
entries.praise: Its automation rules engine is powerful, allowing users to set up complex 'if-this-then-that' logic for shipping methods, package types, and tags.
entries.criticism: The user interface feels dated compared to newer competitors, and navigating its many features can have a steep learning curve for beginners.
entries.criticism_short: Dated interface with a steep learning curve.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Shippo
entries.best_for: Startups and small businesses looking for a simple interface and a flexible, pay-as-you-go pricing model.
entries.verdict: Earns its rank for offering the best flexible pricing model, with a free pay-as-you-go tier that appeals to new businesses and a powerful API for developers.
entries.verdict_short: Top choice for its simple interface, developer-friendly API, and excellent pay-as-you-go pricing.
entries.praise: The API is well-documented and widely considered one of the easiest to integrate for custom shipping solutions, used by platforms like Shopify.
entries.criticism: Customer support on the free tier can be slow, with email response times sometimes exceeding 24 hours during peak periods.
entries.criticism_short: Slow customer support on free plan.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Easyship
entries.best_for: Businesses that ship globally and need to provide customers with accurate, real-time duties and tax calculations at checkout.
entries.verdict: The best choice for international shipping because it automatically calculates and displays duties, taxes, and courier fees in real-time at checkout.
entries.verdict_short: The leader for international shipping, with automated tax/duty calculation and 250+ couriers.
entries.praise: It provides access to over 250 courier services globally, offering more international shipping options than most US-centric competitors.
entries.criticism: The user interface for managing shipments can be slow to load, and some users report discrepancies between quoted and actual final shipping costs.
entries.criticism_short: Slow UI and occasional rate discrepancies.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: ShippingEasy
entries.best_for: Sellers who want to integrate shipping with customer marketing tools, like email campaigns and product reviews.
entries.verdict: Secures its position by combining solid shipping features with unique customer marketing tools, allowing merchants to manage fulfillment and marketing from one platform.
entries.verdict_short: A strong shipping platform with added email marketing and review request features.
entries.praise: Its integration with Constant Contact allows for automated post-purchase email campaigns, a feature not found in most direct competitors.
entries.criticism: The platform is part of the larger Stamps.com portfolio, and some features feel less modern than standalone, venture-backed competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Pirate Ship
entries.best_for: US-based small businesses and Etsy sellers who need the absolute cheapest USPS and UPS rates with no monthly software fees.
entries.verdict: The top option for US-based sellers focused on simplicity and cost, offering access to the deepest USPS and UPS discounts with no monthly software fees.
entries.verdict_short: The best free software for accessing deep USPS and UPS discounts without monthly fees.
entries.praise: Its 'Simple Export Rate' provides exceptionally low costs for international shipments under 4 lbs, often beating all other competitors for that specific segment.
entries.praise_short: Lowest cost for lightweight international shipping.
entries.criticism: It lacks key features for larger businesses, such as advanced automation rules, inventory management, and integrations with carriers beyond USPS and UPS.
entries.criticism_short: No advanced automation or carrier variety.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Veeqo
entries.best_for: Multi-channel sellers, especially on Amazon, who want a powerful and completely free platform for shipping and inventory management.
entries.verdict: This Amazon-owned platform is a top contender because it offers a powerful suite of shipping and inventory tools for free, making it an unbeatable value for multi-channel merchants.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, free shipping and inventory tool, especially valuable for Amazon sellers.
entries.praise: It includes inventory control, purchasing, and reporting features that competitors charge hundreds of dollars per month for.
entries.criticism: As an Amazon product, its primary focus is on serving Amazon sellers, and support for non-Amazon channels can feel like a lower priority.
entries.criticism_short: Focus is heavily on the Amazon ecosystem.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Sendcloud
entries.best_for: European businesses needing a shipping platform with deep integrations into local European carriers and fulfillment networks.
entries.verdict: The best shipping software for European merchants, offering connections to over 80 carriers in the region, including local leaders like DPD, Colissimo, and PostNL.
entries.verdict_short: The leading shipping platform for Europe with extensive local carrier integrations.
entries.praise: Its branded tracking pages and returns portal are highly customizable and support multiple languages, improving the customer experience for pan-European stores.
entries.praise_short: Excellent multi-language tracking and returns portal.
entries.criticism: While it operates in the US, its carrier options and support are much more limited compared to its deep network within the European Union.
entries.criticism_short: Limited carrier options and support in the US.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: Ordoro
entries.best_for: High-growth businesses that need to manage not just shipping, but also inventory, purchasing, and dropshipping from a single platform.
entries.verdict: Stands out by bundling shipping management with advanced inventory and dropshipping features, making it a true command center for ecommerce operations.
entries.verdict_short: An operations platform combining shipping, inventory, and dropshipping management.
entries.praise: Its kitting and bundling features are superior to most shipping-focused platforms, allowing for accurate inventory tracking of products sold in sets.
entries.praise_short: Strong inventory kitting and bundling features.
entries.criticism: The user interface is less intuitive than competitors like Shippo, and its pricing is higher, putting it out of reach for many small businesses.
entries.criticism_short: Less intuitive UI and higher starting price.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: ShipWorks
entries.best_for: High-volume warehouses that require the speed and reliability of a desktop-based application for processing thousands of orders daily.
entries.verdict: This desktop-based software is built for speed and power, making it a preferred choice for warehouse environments that prioritize processing speed over a modern web interface.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful desktop application designed for high-volume warehouse shipping operations.
entries.praise: Because it runs locally on a Windows PC, it can process batches of thousands of labels faster than most web-based applications.
entries.praise_short: Fast batch processing for large order volumes.
entries.criticism: It requires a Windows-based computer for installation and lacks the flexibility of web-based software that can be accessed from any device.
entries.criticism_short: Requires Windows PC, no web access.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: AfterShip
entries.best_for: Brands focused on the post-purchase customer experience, offering best-in-class order tracking and returns management.
entries.verdict: Makes the list for its exceptional post-purchase features, providing the market's leading solution for branded tracking pages, delivery notifications, and returns automation.
entries.verdict_short: Best-in-class for branded order tracking pages and returns management portals.
entries.praise: Its tracking API connects to over 1,000 carriers worldwide, providing more consistent and reliable tracking data than almost any other platform.
entries.praise_short: Connects to over 1,000 carriers for tracking.
entries.criticism: Its core label-printing and shipping management module (AfterShip Shipping) is less developed and has fewer features than its tracking and returns products.
entries.criticism_short: Core shipping features are less developed.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.

entries.name: EasyPost
entries.best_for: Developers and large businesses that want to build custom shipping functionality directly into their own software or ERP system.
entries.verdict: The wildcard pick is an API-first platform for developers, not a traditional shipping dashboard, making it ideal for businesses wanting to build custom shipping logic directly into their applications.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, flexible API for developers to build custom shipping solutions.
entries.praise: Its unified API allows access to over 100 carriers with a single integration, saving hundreds of developer hours compared to building individual carrier connections.
entries.criticism: It is not an out-of-the-box solution for non-technical users; it requires a developer to implement and has no native user interface for managing day-to-day shipments.
entries.criticism_short: Requires a developer; no user interface.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-12.
